comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Post-1fa8 note: 1fa8 shipped infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md — S6's back-out section must EXTEND that runbook (add the feature-branch ACL revoke + submit-type pin revert + bot-image rollback) rather than create a parallel document.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: AC3 BACK-OUT TESTED LIVE (reversible). (1) ACL revoke restores single-change flow — TESTED: pushed refs/meta/config removing 'pushMerge = group feature-branch-drivers' from [access refs/for/refs/heads/main] (keeping exclusiveGroupPermissions so no inherited leak); a --no-ff merge push to refs/for/main by JoeOakhartNava (a feature-branch-driver who merged change 254 earlier) was then REFUSED 'commit 7451b07: you are not allowed to upload merges' -> single-change-only restored. Then restored the grant via a forward commit (project.config byte-identical to snapshot f8bd6ac8; both pushMerge grants back); a merge push SUCCEEDS again (change 282, abandoned). Fully reversible, zero residual config change. (2) Submit-type revert VERIFIED vs S1 prior — LIVE read GET /a/projects/rebar/config: submit_type=MERGE_IF_NECESSARY, use_content_merge configured=TRUE/inherited=True -> matches S1-recorded MERGE_IF_NECESSARY + content-merge true, so deleting the [submit] block is a proven no-op. (3) Bot rollback path DOCUMENTED (two-vote-gate-rollback.md §A.2 pt4 + review-bot-ops.md :prev rollback) — and exercised live this session (the review-bot fix, bug pelt-mead-aeon). Note: setup-project.sh (audited apply path) needs an admin SSH key absent here, so the test used a direct admin HTTPS refs/meta/config push + snapshot restore.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Epic 88ab (elfish-chair-lug) S6. The pattern's known failure mode is branch rot (OpenDev warns feature branches are "not for sustained long term development"; Qt abandoned routine long-lived-branch merges) — so lifetime/cadence policy and a tested back-out must exist, and the cost claims (re-reviews avoided vs CI re-runs incurred) must be measured, not asserted.

## What
- Branch-lifetime cap + catch-up merge cadence documented (policy lives with the S4 CONTRIBUTING section; this story owns the policy content + the inventory tooling).
- Stale-branch inventory: a scripted check (infra/scripts or infra/gerrit) flags `feature/*` branches inactive beyond the cap — merged-back OR abandoned — for owner-confirmed deletion; recorded in the runbook.
- Back-out documented + TESTED on the live Gerrit: revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow ("no label/gate changes REQUIRED" — the copyCondition addition is inert absent merge changes and may be left or reverted); submit-type pin back-out = remove the [submit] block and confirm the inherited value matches the S1-recorded prior state; bot code back-out = redeploy the prior review-bot image.
- "Cost & latency" section in the amending ADR, from the S5 runs: median CI wall-clock per merge change; count of LLM re-reviews avoided via MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE carry vs CI re-runs incurred across scenarios; per-review LLM cost if surfaced by the runner.

## Scope
Policy + tooling + measurements. Depends on S5 (run data) and the S1-recorded submit-type prior state. No new gate/bot behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lifetime cap + cadence policy written and referenced from CONTRIBUTING/runbook
- [ ] Stale-branch inventory script committed + runbook entry (flag + owner-confirmed delete; covers abandoned branches)
- [ ] Back-out tested live: ACL revoke restores single-change flow; submit-type revert verified against the S1-recorded value; bot rollback path documented
- [ ] ADR "Cost & latency" section populated with the three named metrics from S5 data
compiled_state.title: S6: Process policy + back-out + cost/latency record for the feature-branch flow
